rationale.R1.2: Excessive hedging ('seemed to work fairly well', 'appears to perform reasonably well') undermines the internal logical consistency of the claims; the argument structure vacillates between asserting results and disavowing them, creating inferential gaps.
rationale.R1.3: WER comparisons to 'several recently reported numbers' are unanchored to specific results; the SpecAugment ablation reports a magnitude (~0.6 WER) without baseline/controlled-condition precision, weakening claim-to-evidence traceability.
rationale.R1.6: The dominant pathology in this artifact is writing quality: pervasive over-hedging ('possibly promising', 'tentatively interpret', 'somewhat effective') dilutes concrete results and makes the section read as uncertain about its own contributions.
rationale.R1.7: Prior-work comparisons lack citations; 'recently reported numbers in the literature' is unattributed, making it impossible to verify the comparative claims or assign credit.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily seeking writing-level edits (tightening prose, eliminating excessive hedging, improving sentence quality), or a substantive technical review (verifying claim-evidence alignment, auditing citations, and assessing logical consistency of the reported results)?
